data.body: Bug-sweep triage: RC1 (shallow-rebase SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT) + RC2a (fsck ORPHAN false positive) are FIXED by commit 5a7548a0e (rebar-ticket: f193; RED->GREEN + mutation-tested tests), which PREVENTS new corruption. However the completion-verifier correctly FAILS an honest close on 3 unmet criteria that were deferred to epic ace2-f784-4b19-4f22: (1) no test reproduces the actual concurrent-writers+compaction attack vector (shipped tests assert workflow CONFIG invariants + fsck unit logic, not a real interleaving); (2) flock does not cover the cross-machine merge-during-push critical section (the fix is the CI merge-not-rebase change, not a lock); (3) no store remediation / fsck --repair exists to drive the accumulated corruption count to zero. RECOMMEND: treat f193 as superseded by epic ace2 (which owns remediation + hardening + a true repro test), or keep open tracked under ace2. NOT force-closing as 'done' — that would fake a certification the residual scope doesn't meet.
